page: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-32GB is a compute-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 200 GB of local SSD storage.

Benchmark evaluations reveal distinct performance characteristics. The server demonstrates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12262.43 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9230.0. However, single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core performance falling into the bottom 10% at 786.69. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small cached blocks (834.03 GB/sec) but drop to weak levels (bottom 25%) for large uncached read and write operations, measuring 28.32 GB/sec and 16.09 GB/sec respectively.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offer a dense compute profile without the premium of dedicated cores. This makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded workloads that can tolerate shared CPU resources, such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and static web serving. It is less suitable for single-threaded tasks or memory-intensive applications requiring high uncached memory throughput.
description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-32GB is a compute-optimized server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 16 shared vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 200 GB of local SSD storage. While multi-core benchmarks like Geekbench and stress-ng show average performance, single-core CPU performance is poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also weak, though small cached block reads remain average. This hardware profile is best suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression, where parallel processing can offset the shared CPU allocation and lower single-core speeds.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-32GB is a compute-optimized KVM server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD storage, offering average multi-core performance but limited single-core speeds.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-16xCPU-32GB is a KVM server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring sixteen shared AMD EPYC cores and local SSD storage for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 32 GB of system memory.
200 GB of local SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address.
Average multi-core performance in Geekbench and stress-ng benchmarks.
Weak uncached memory bandwidth and poor single-core CPU performance.
Suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
